issues.description: verification-gate was executed in-process (xcodebuild build + xcodebuild test ran inline, output reviewed, PASS determined) but the Skill tool was never invoked. No announce string ('Using verification-gate to ...'), no TodoWrite checklist expansion, and no formal PASS/FAIL evidence block was produced. The required verification work happened — the discipline mechanism did not. Guard 5 (In-process chain execution) requires the Skill tool to be invoked even when executing inline.
issues.detected_by: retrospective review of tool call sequence

issues.description: acceptance-contract was not invoked before coding-agent. The task was a pure visual reskin (no behavioral acceptance criteria that would require a failing test written before coding). However, the skill should have been explicitly considered and waived with a stated reason rather than silently skipped. The orchestrator's checklist includes acceptance-contract for 'issues with acceptance criteria'; a quick waiver note would have been correct.

project-state-updater was not invoked as a formal skill after verification passed. The project-state.md update was done inline by coding-agent (which is within coding-agent's explicit mandate: 'append a structured entry to docs/ai/project-state.md before handoff to verification-gate'). This is a partial W1 — the update happened but the dedicated skill was bypassed. Low severity for this run since coding-agent's inline update is spec-compliant.

workflow_adherence.notes: The substance of the work was correct — all 8 test suites passed, build green, no logic changed, project state updated, draft PR opened. The chain discipline gaps (verification-gate not formally entered, acceptance-contract silently skipped) are procedural. Primary fix target: enforce verification-gate skill invocation even in-process, per Guard 5.
